content.problemStatement:
In order to protect the southern border of Bytelandian Empire against intrusions by various nomadic groups, the Emperor of Byteland has decided to build a wall along the southern border. The best architect Johny is recruited for the task.


In order to minimise the cost of raw material, Johny is restricted to use only following two kinds of building blocks:
X      #

XX

The height of the wall is fixed and is 2 units, but the length of the wall varies. As a part of his job Johny needs to find out the number of ways he can construct the wall using above two types of building blocks where the length of the wall is specified. Write a program to help Johny.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^9) – the length of the wall.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of ways to build a 2×N wall using the given blocks, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 only a vertical block fits → 1 way. N=2 can be two vertical blocks or one 2×2 block → 2 ways. The recurrence f(N)=f(N-1)+f(N-2) yields the sequence 1,2,3,5,8.
Continuing the recurrence: f(6)=13, f(7)=21, f(8)=34.

solution.approach: The wall can be tiled using either a vertical 2×1 block (covers one column) or a 2×2 block (covers two columns). Let f(N) be the number of ways to tile a 2×N wall. For the first column we either place a vertical block and then tile the remaining 2×(N‑1) wall, or we place a 2×2 block and then tile the remaining 2×(N‑2) wall. Hence f(N) = f(N‑1) + f(N‑2) with base values f(1)=1, f(2)=2. This is exactly the Fibonacci sequence shifted by one: f(N) = F_{N+1} where F_1 = 1, F_2 = 1. To handle N up to 10^9 we compute F_{N+1} modulo 1e9+7 using the fast‑doubling method, which returns (F_n, F_{n+1}) in O(log n) time. The algorithm is recursive, uses only a few modular multiplications, and fits easily within the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log N) – each test case requires O(log N) operations due to fast doubling.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log N) – recursion depth of the fast‑doubling routine.
